title: Free Things to Do With Kids in Tauranga
meta_desc: The best free and cheap things to do with kids in Tauranga and Mount Maunganui — beaches, the Mount base track, estuary walks and parks. Family-tested favourites.
intro: Tauranga and Mount Maunganui are a family beach paradise, and the very best of it — the sand, the surf, the coastal walks — is free. We've spent countless sunny days here circling the Mount base track, swimming at Pilot Bay, and exploring the estuary walkways without spending a cent. Here are our favourite free and cheap things to do with kids in Tauranga and the Mount.

sections.heading: Beaches & the Mount
sections.body:
Mount Maunganui Main Beach is one of NZ's best, with patrolled swimming in summer, soft sand and gentle Pilot Bay on the harbour side for toddlers. The free walk around the base of Mauao (the Mount) is a 45-minute flat loop with stunning coastal views — easy enough for school-age kids and pram-friendly in parts. The fitter and braver can climb to the summit for free.

Papamoa Beach stretches for kilometres of free, uncrowded sand a little further east — perfect for a relaxed family day.
list: Mauao (Mount) base track — free 45-min flat coastal loop
Mount Main Beach — patrolled swimming, soft sand
Pilot Bay — calm harbour-side beach, great for toddlers
Papamoa Beach — kilometres of free, quieter sand

sections.heading: Estuary & Coastal Walks
sections.body: The Waikareao Estuary Walkway is a free, flat 9km loop (do a shorter section with little legs) with boardwalks over the water and loads of birdlife — a lovely, sheltered option on a windy day. Closer to the city, the harbourside paths and the Tauranga waterfront are free to wander with playgrounds dotted along the way.
list: Waikareao Estuary Walkway — free boardwalks, birdlife, flat
Tauranga waterfront — free harbourside paths and playgrounds
Memorial Park — free playground, splash pad and harbour pool area
Yatton Park — free shaded green space with big old trees

sections.heading: Parks, Waterfalls & Splash Pads
sections.body: McLaren Falls Park, about 15 minutes from the city, is free to enter and a magic spot for families — waterfalls, lakeside picnic lawns, easy walks and glow-worms after dark. Memorial Park's splash pad and the various suburban playgrounds give kids free water play and run-around space, and the harbour-edge Memorial Park pool area is a local favourite in summer.
list: McLaren Falls Park — free entry, waterfalls, picnics, glow-worms
Memorial Park splash pad — free summer water play
Fergusson Park — free waterfront playground and walks
Robbins Park & Rose Gardens — free gardens with city views

sections.heading: Cheap Treats & Rainy-Day Options
sections.body: Baywave is the local aquatic centre with a wave pool, hydroslide and toddler pool — not free but cheap and a brilliant wet-weather option. The Tauranga Historic Village charges a small entry to step back in time, and the central library runs free school-holiday programmes. For a free animal fix, the estuary and beaches are full of seabirds and the occasional dolphin pod offshore.
list: Baywave — wave pool and hydroslide, small entry (great rainy-day)
Tauranga Historic Village — small entry, hands-on heritage
Tauranga library — free play and holiday programmes
Beach and estuary birdwatching — free

sections.heading: Budget Summary
sections.body: With some of the country's best beaches and coastal walks all free, Tauranga and the Mount make it easy to have a brilliant family day for almost nothing. Here's how a free day adds up for a family of four.
list: Mount base track & Main Beach: FREE
Waikareao Estuary Walkway: FREE
McLaren Falls Park: FREE
Parking (most beaches/parks): FREE; metered near the Mount in summer
Pack-your-own picnic: ~$20 for a family of four
Estimated full-day cost: $0–$25
